revision.text:
[[de:Enoshima-Schrein]][[en:Enoshima Shrine]][[fr:Enoshima-jinja]][[ja:江島神社]][[ko:에노시마 신사]][[zh:江島神社]] {{Short description|Shrine in Fujisawa, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{more footnotes|date=June 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Enoshima Jinja
江島神社 | image = File:Enoshimajinja -05.jpg | caption = Zuishinmon | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.30036111111111|N|139.4796111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|qid=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} | founded_by = | established = 552 | date_destroyed = | location = 2-3-8, Enoshima, Fujisawa-shi, Kanagawa prefecture, Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.enoshimajinja.or.jp/index.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Fujisawa|qid=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} | part_of = {{ill|Q17225980|qid=Q17225980|1=Kamakura Enoshima Shichifukujin}}; {{ill|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|qid=Q17214976|1=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten}} }} [[File:Okutsuno-miya Enoshima Shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|left|Oku-tsu-miya (奧津宮)|alt=]] '''Enoshima Shrine''' (江島神社) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|Enoshima|en|Enoshima|qid=Q989803|lt=Enoshima|1=Enoshima}}, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa, Kanagawa|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, Japan. The shrine is dedicated to the worship of the ''[[kami]]'' [[:en:Benten|Benten]].{{cite book|title=Shinto the Kami Way|author1=Ono, S.|author2=Woodard, W.P.|author3=Sakamoto, S.|date=2004|publisher=Tuttle Publishing|isbn=978-0-8048-3557-2|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=KhFXB_X7AA4C|page=101}} Enoshima-jinja comprises three shrines, He-tsu-miya, Naka-tsu-miya and Oku-tsu-miya. According to legend, 12th-century Japanese ruler {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|qid=Q589811|lt=Hōjō Tokimasa|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} visited the shrine to pray for prosperity, and there heard a prophecy from a mysterious woman, who left behind three scales, which became his family crest.{{cite book|last=Chaudhuri|first=Saroj Kumar|title=Hindu Gods and Goddesses in Japan|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=vhAZEZD7weoC&pg=PA51|year=2003|publisher=Vedams eBooks (P) Ltd|isbn=978-81-7936-009-5|page=51}} ==Overview== {{refimprove|time=2019-06-29T12:59:50+00:00}} {{Japan Shrine | name = Enoshima Shrine | img = File:Enoshimajinja -05.jpg | img_capt = Zuishinmon Gate | image_width = 250px | nihongo = 江島神社 | hiragana = えのしまじんじゃ | romaji = | location = {{JPN}}{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Enoshima|en|Enoshima|qid=Q989803|lt=Enoshima|1=Enoshima}} 2-3-8 | god = [[Tagirihime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Prefectural Shrine|Prefectural Shrine]], [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Beppyo Shrine]] | struct = {{link-ja|Gongen-zukuri|権現造}} (Hetsunomiya, Nakatsunomiya)
[[Irimoya-zukuri]] (Okutsunomiya) | open_year = 13th year of Emperor Kinmei (552) | cere_date = First Serpent Day of April | title = Kamakura Enoshima Shichifukujin (Benzaiten) |}} '''Enoshima Shrine''' ({{jpn|j=江島神社|hg=えのしまじんじゃ|rm=|f=y}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on {{ill|Enoshima|en|Enoshima|qid=Q989803|lt=Enoshima|1=Enoshima}}, [[Fujisawa City]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is currently a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Beppyo Shrine]] under the administration of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and is recognized as one of Japan's three major Benzaiten shrines{{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|qid=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island|1=Chikubu Island}} (Tsukubusuma Shrine), [[Itsukushima]] (Itsukushima Shrine), and Enoshima (Enoshima Shrine) are Japan's three major Benzaiten shrines.. == Deities == [[File:Benzaiten_Stele_(Enoshima_Shrine).JPG|thumb|150x150px|Statue of Benzaiten and her child]] The shrine primarily enshrines the three Munakata goddesses. The "Okutsunomiya" located to the west of Enoshima enshrines [[Tagirihime-no-Mikoto]], the "Nakatsunomiya" in the center enshrines [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], and the "Hetsunomiya" to the north enshrines [[Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto]], collectively known as "Enoshima Ōkami". During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the enshrined [[Benzaiten]] was referred to as '''Enoshima Benzaiten''' or '''Enoshima Myōjin'''. == Buildings == [[File:Enoshimajinja_-01.jpg|thumb|120x120px|Okutsunomiya (March 27, 2011)]] [[File:Enoshimajinja_-02.jpg|thumb|120x120px|Nakatsunomiya (March 27, 2011)]] [[File:Enoshimajinja_-03.jpg|thumb|120x120px|Hetsunomiya (March 27, 2011)]] [[File:Enoshimajinja_-04.jpg|thumb|120x120px|Hōanden (Benten Hall)]] * Okutsunomiya: The former main shrine. Enshrines Tagirihime-no-Mikoto. The main hall was originally built in the 13th year of the [[:en:Tenpō era|Tenpō era]], and rebuilt in 1842. * Nakatsunomiya: The former upper shrine. Enshrines Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto. Originally built in the 3rd year of the [[Ninju era]] (853), and rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1689). * Hetsunomiya: The former lower shrine. Enshrines Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto. Built in the 1st year of the [[Ken’ei era]] (1206) by [[Minamoto no Sanetomo]]. The current main hall was rebuilt in the 3rd year of the [[Empō era]] (1675). * Hōanden (Benten Hall): Enshrines the eight-armed Benzaiten and Myoon Benzaiten. * Auxiliary Shrines ** [[Yasaka Shrine]]: Enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. The main hall was rebuilt in the 1st year of the [[Kōka era]] (1844). ** Ryūgū Shrine: Enshrines Ryūgū Daijin. ** {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} ** [[Akiba Shrines]] * Zuishinmon Gate: Modeled after Ryūgū-jō, the Dragon Palace. * Bronze Torii: A bronze [[torii]] gate at the entrance to the shrine grounds, located near the entrance to Enoshima. === Cultural Properties === ==== Important Cultural Properties of Japan ==== * Wooden Colored Seated Statue of Eight-armed Benzaiten (Sculpture) [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1414437.html 「文化審議会答申〜国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について〜」] {{Wayback|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1414437.html |date=20200815053000 }}（Japanese Agency for Cultural Affairs, March 18, 2019）Notice No. 26 of the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, July 23, 2019 ==== Others ==== * Sword made by Fujiwara Masahiro from {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|qid=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}}: Prefectural Designation (Craft) * Turtle Gazing in All Directions ({{lang|ja|八方睨みの亀}}): City Designation (Painting) * Enoshima Engi: City Designation (Calligraphy) * Bronze Torii: City Designation Cultural Property (Building) == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Enoshima|en|Enoshima|qid=Q989803|lt=Enoshima|1=Enoshima}} * [[Benzaiten]] == External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20130630101829/http://www.enoshimajinja.or.jp/index.html Enoshima Shrine] (Official Website) == See also == *{{ill|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|en|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|qid=Q17214976|lt=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|1=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated
